text.title: API GraphQL Design Output Contract
text.summary: JSON Schema (draft-07) pinning the shape of a valid API GraphQL Design artefact. The validator script enforces required keys, types, and the forbidden-pattern list before the orchestrator accepts the output.

section.title: Schema

example.code: { "artefact_id": "api-graphql-2026-05-23", "owner": "ruslan@faion.net", "last_touched": "2026-05-23T12:00:00Z", "template_version": "1.1.0", "status": "ready_for_review", "evidence": [ { "source": "https://example.com/source-1", "citation": "verbatim quote from source" } ], "api_id": "draft", "schema_path": "draft", "dataloader_required": true, "pagination_style": "draft", "depth_limit": 1, "complexity_limit": 1, "persisted_queries": true, "introspection_in_prod": true }
example.note: Minimum viable conformant output — every required field present, no forbidden pattern matched. Used as the `--self-test` fixture by `scripts/validate-api-graphql.py`.
Missing every required field; no owner, no template_version, no evidence array — rejected by `validate-api-graphql.py` with exit 1.

forbidden.pattern: Field declared as list/object in the schema is emitted as prose paragraph.
`template_version` empty or set to a string that does not match the methodology frontmatter at write time.
`evidence` array empty for any non-trivial field declared in the schema.
Agent self-approves the artefact (`status=approved`) without a named human reviewer distinct from the producing agent.

text.reference: Schema validator (stdlib only). Exit 0 on pass, 1 on fail with violation list.
